exceptions.rationale: NXP Monkey already passes Pyright standard mode across source and tests. This release keeps behavior unchanged while the public KEX XML/JSON boundary types and fixture-heavy tests are reviewed before enabling strict mode.
exceptions.review: Move to strict mode after the data-boundary Any usage has explicit TypedDict or dataclass ownership.

exceptions.rule: research docs excluded from release artifacts
exceptions.rationale: The XML survey is useful repository reference material for KEX file-format work, but this release keeps research documents out of the source distribution per ADR-0001.
exceptions.review: Promote durable file-format material into docs/canonical_format or docs/design before making it part of release artifacts.

exceptions.rationale: The package already ships from src/py and downstream references use that layout. Moving it during a release-hardening pass would create avoidable churn.
exceptions.review: Consider migrating to src/nxp_monkey during a dedicated packaging cleanup release.

exceptions.rule: strict complexity, annotation, and Any usage limits
exceptions.rationale: Several KEX parser, fetch, and CLI wrapper functions predate the standards repo. This release records the current baseline and blocks growth in complexity, function size, missing annotations, and Any usage.
exceptions.review: Refactor baseline offenders down over time. Baseline updates should reduce counts or include explicit release-note rationale.
